verses.2: Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD,
verses.3: Blessed are those who keep justice.
verses.4: Remember me, LORD, with the favor that you show to your people.
verses.5: that I may see the prosperity of your chosen,
verses.6: We have sinned with our fathers.
verses.7: Our fathers didn’t understand your wonders in Egypt.
verses.8: Nevertheless he saved them for his name’s sake,
verses.9: He rebuked the Red Sea also, and it was dried up;
verses.10: He saved them from the hand of him who hated them,
verses.14: but gave in to craving in the desert,
verses.16: They envied Moses also in the camp,
verses.17: The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan,
verses.18: A fire was kindled in their company.
verses.19: They made a calf in Horeb,
verses.22: wondrous works in the land of Ham,
verses.23: Therefore he said that he would destroy them,
verses.24: Yes, they despised the pleasant land.
verses.27: that he would overthrow their offspring among the nations,
verses.28: They joined themselves also to Baal Peor,
verses.29: Thus they provoked him to anger with their deeds.
verses.30: Then Phinehas stood up and executed judgment,
verses.31: That was credited to him for righteousness,
verses.32: They angered him also at the waters of Meribah,
verses.33: because they were rebellious against his spirit,
verses.35: but mixed themselves with the nations,
verses.37: Yes, they sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons.
verses.39: Thus they were defiled with their works,
verses.40: Therefore the LORD burned with anger against his people.
verses.41: He gave them into the hand of the nations.
verses.45: He remembered for them his covenant,
verses.46: He made them also to be pitied
verses.48: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel,
